title: Pilosella plicatula
intro: Pilosella plicatula, also known as the pleated hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its distinctive, pleated leaves and small yellow flowers. Traditionally, Pilosella plicatula has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe the gastrointestinal tract and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pilosulin and 11β-hydroxypilosulin, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Pilosella plicatula is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which may be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
taxonomy: Pilosella plicatula is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, a group of plants closely related to the genus Crepis. The species Pilosella plicatula is further categorized within the subgenus Eunomius. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family. Pilosella plicatula is native to Europe and is a rare species found in mountainous regions.
uses: Pilosella plicatula is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Some studies suggest that Pilosella plicatula extracts have potential in treating various conditions, including diabetes and cardiovascular diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal benefits. The plant's traditional uses vary across different regions, highlighting the need for further investigation into its pharmacological properties.
